analyst_observations: Inspector Report and Caveman style are the dominant differentiators — baseline reliably misses both.
Both configurations land the deliverable content (SQL, table, A/B copy). The skill's value-add is the wrapper: routing visibility, QA pass, terse chat.

One assertion fails in BOTH configs (a6 'recommend which version to send first' on writing eval) — that's a signal the assertion may be miscalibrated, OR the skill should explicitly require a 'pick one' recommendation in writing tasks. Worth user input.
With-skill chat prose is mostly caveman but slips into normal prose in dense synthesis sections (research eval). Could tighten skill instructions on this.
Tokens and time are roughly comparable — the skill doesn't introduce meaningful overhead.
